nouns.summary: The verse frames the person’s עֲמַל as something that is connected to what he will experience afterward, leading into the statement about his soul’s outcome.
The נַפְשׁ is described as seeing and being satisfied as a result of the person’s experience, with the following clause linking this to his knowledge.
The verse says that the individual will be satisfied בְּדַעְתֹּו, meaning his knowledge is what brings about the satisfaction.

The צַדִּיק is presented as an agent in the outcome of justification, appearing in the phrase that connects righteousness to the act of justifying others (through the servant mentioned in the same construction).
The עֶבֶד is characterized as being justified/approved in relation to many, and the verse also states that he will bear the iniquities.
The לַרַבִּים phrase places the act of justification in relation to many people, indicating that the righteous-serving outcome extends to a large group.
The verse directly associates עֲוֹנוֹת with being carried: the person is said to יִסְבֹּל (bear) their iniquities.
